description: 5-card hand system: deal 7, keep 5, with hand-in-score tie condition. Reduces hand from 10 to 5 cards, adds a lightweight draft phase (pick 5 of 7 dealt cards), and introduces hand-in-score (owned cells + cards in hand) so ties are naturally reachable and Sudden Death becomes a real game mechanic. Player who goes first places all 5 cards; player who goes second places 4 and keeps 1 in hand (+1 to their score). Tie at 5-5 triggers Sudden Death. Includes backend rules, draft endpoint, frontend draft UI, and updated rules dialog.

userStories.title: Add DRAFTING game status and PlayerState.deal field
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the game model to support a drafting phase â€” so the system can track the 7 dealt cards separately from the 5-card hand the player keeps.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `DRAFTING = "drafting"` to `GameStatus` enum in `app/models/game.py`
Add `deal: list[str] = []` field to `PlayerState` in `app/models/game.py` â€” holds the 7 dealt card keys before draft selection
Add constants `DEAL_SIZE = 7` and `HAND_SIZE = 5` to `app/rules/deck.py`
Existing serialization/deserialization of `GameState` still works (deal defaults to empty list for old snapshots)
`uv run pytest` passes with no regressions
`uv run pyright` passes

userStories.title: Change deck generation from 10-card decks to 7-card deals
userStories.description: As a game system, I want to generate two balanced 7-card deals instead of 10-card decks â€” so each player gets 7 cards to draft from.
acceptanceCriteria: Rename `generate_matched_decks` to `generate_matched_deals` in `app/rules/deck.py`; update all call sites
Change generation target from 10 cards per side to `DEAL_SIZE` (7) cards per side
Update `_can_add_to_deck` cap from 10 to `DEAL_SIZE`
Rename `validate_deck` to `validate_deal`; change size check from 10 to `DEAL_SIZE`; update all call sites
Keep rarity slot limits unchanged (ultraâ‰¤1, very_rareâ‰¤2, rareâ‰¤3 â€” all valid for 7 cards)
Update `tests/test_deck_generation.py` and `tests/test_deck_validator.py` for 7-card deals
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: Draft phase: join_game sets DRAFTING, new submit_draft service
userStories.description: As a player, when I join a game I want to receive 7 cards and choose 5 to keep â€” so I have a meaningful decision before the match begins.
acceptanceCriteria: In `game_service.py` `join_game`: when 2nd player joins, generate 7-card deals, assign to each player's `deal` field, and set status to `DRAFTING` (not `ACTIVE`)
Board elements and starting player are still determined at join time (same as current behavior)
New `submit_draft(game_store, game_id, player_id, selected_cards)` function in `game_service.py`
Validates `selected_cards` is exactly 5 card keys, all present in that player's `deal`
Sets the player's `hand` to the 5 selected cards and clears their `deal` to `[]`
When both players have submitted drafts (both `hand` lengths == 5 and both `deal` lengths == 0), transition status to `ACTIVE`
Emits `draft_submitted` event via `game_store.append_event`
Raises appropriate errors: game not in DRAFTING status, player not in game, wrong number of cards, cards not in deal
New tests in `tests/test_draft.py`: valid submission, both-submitted transition to ACTIVE, invalid card count, card not in deal, duplicate submission
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: Draft endpoint: POST /games/{game_id}/draft
userStories.description: As a frontend client, I want an API endpoint to submit my 5-card draft selection â€” so the drafting phase can be completed over HTTP.
acceptanceCriteria: New `POST /games/{game_id}/draft` endpoint in `app/routers/games.py`
Request body: `{ "selected_cards": ["key1", "key2", "key3", "key4", "key5"] }`
Calls `submit_draft` from game_service; returns the updated `GameState`
Returns 404 if game not found, 403 if player not in game, 422 if validation fails, 409 on version conflict
Auth: requires valid JWT (same pattern as existing endpoints)
Add router-level tests or integration tests verifying status codes
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: Hand-in-score: update scoring to include cards in hand
userStories.description: As a player, I want the final score to count owned board cells plus cards remaining in my hand â€” so ties are possible and going second has a built-in compensation.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `compute_round_result` in `app/rules/reducer.py` to accept `players` parameter
New scoring: player score = cells owned on board + len(player.hand)
Update the call site in `apply_intent` to pass the updated players list (after hand removal) to `compute_round_result`
Tie (equal scores) still returns `is_draw=True`
With 5-card hands: P1 (goes first, places 5) ends with 0 in hand; P2 (goes second, places 4) ends with 1 in hand; tie occurs when P1 owns 5 cells and P2 owns 4 cells
Update `tests/test_scoring.py` with hand-in-score test cases: P1 wins at 6+ cells, P2 wins at 5+ cells, tie at P1=5/P2=4
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: Update early finish check for hand-in-score
userStories.description: As the rules engine, I want the early finish optimization to account for hand-in-score â€” so games are correctly called early with the new scoring model.
acceptanceCriteria: In `apply_intent`, update the early finish check to account for cards remaining in each player's hand
A player has won early if their minimum possible final score exceeds the opponent's maximum possible final score
The second player (who will end with 1 card in hand) gets +1 factored into the calculation
Add test cases: early finish triggers correctly under hand-in-score, early finish does NOT trigger when hand bonus could change the outcome
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: Update Sudden Death hand rebuild for hand-in-score
userStories.description: As the rules engine, I want Sudden Death to rebuild hands correctly â€” each player gets back the board cards they own plus any cards still in their hand, giving both players 5 cards for the next round.
acceptanceCriteria: In `begin_sudden_death_round`, rebuild each player's hand as: cards they own on the board + cards remaining in their hand
On a tie (P1 owns 5 cells + 0 in hand, P2 owns 4 cells + 1 in hand), both players get exactly 5 cards back
Update `tests/test_sudden_death.py`: remove the 'mathematically impossible' note, add tests that verify hand rebuild includes hand cards, verify both players get 5 cards on a standard tie
SD cap at 3 rounds still works
`uv run pytest` passes

userStories.title: Frontend: drafting phase UI
userStories.description: As a player, I want a card selection screen after joining a game â€” so I can pick 5 of my 7 dealt cards before the match begins.
acceptanceCriteria: In `GameRoom.tsx`, add a `"drafting"` status branch that renders a `DraftingGameView` component
New `DraftingGameView` component shows the player's 7 dealt cards (reuse card styling from HandPanel)
Player can toggle cards on/off; maximum 5 selected, minimum 5 required to confirm
Selected cards have a distinct visual state (e.g. highlighted border, lifted); unselected cards appear dimmed
A 'Confirm' button submits the 5 selected cards via `POST /games/{game_id}/draft`
After submission, show a waiting state ('Waiting for opponent to draft...')
If the opponent has already drafted, the game transitions to ACTIVE automatically (realtime subscription picks up the event)
Non-participant viewers in DRAFTING see a 'Players are drafting...' message
Add a frontend test: render DraftingGameView, verify 7 cards shown, select 5, confirm button enabled
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Update rules dialog and scoring display for new system
userStories.description: As a player, I want the in-game rules dialog and score display to reflect the new 5-card hand and hand-in-score system â€” so the rules I read match how the game actually works.
acceptanceCriteria: In `GameRulesDialog.tsx`, update the 'Goal' section to explain hand-in-score: 'Your score is the number of cards you control on the board plus cards remaining in your hand'
Add a 'Draft Phase' section explaining deal 7, keep 5
Update the 'Winning' section to mention ties are possible and trigger Sudden Death
Add a 'Sudden Death' section explaining: on tie, each player gets back their owned cards + hand cards, board resets, max 3 SD rounds then draw
Update the 'Your Turn' section to mention the second player keeps 1 card in hand as a score bonus
If the game room shows a score counter, update it to reflect hand-in-score (owned cells + hand size)
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Documentation refresh: CLAUDE.md and game rules docs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CLAUDE.md and GAME_RULES_OVERVIEW.md updated to reflect the new 5-card hand system â€” so documentation matches the implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `CLAUDE.md` 'Key Domain Concepts' section: change 'Deck (10 cards)' to describe deal 7, keep 5
Update `CLAUDE.md` Sudden Death description to note it is now reachable via hand-in-score ties
Update `docs/GAME_RULES_OVERVIEW.md` to reflect new hand size, draft phase, and scoring
No stale references to '10-card deck' or 'hand of 10' remain in documentation files
